title: Sensorium Directive Outcome v1
description: Audit-only outcome record for a sensorium-directive.v1. Exactly one outcome record exists for every directive, including rejected, failed, timed_out, and completed directives. Outcome records are not published to local Agora observation topics and are reachable only through host-owned audit capabilities.

schema.description: Schema tag for the v1 Sensorium contract.

outcome/id.description: Opaque audit outcome id assigned by sensorium-core; recommended to be ULID-prefixed.

correlation/id.description: Optional opaque id threading this directive outcome through a higher-level plan or workflow.

outcome/status.description: Directive outcome status. admitted is used for async acceptance before final execution result is known.

outcome/recorded_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which sensorium-core wrote the outcome record.

directive/issued_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp copied from the directive when available.

started_at.description: Connector-reported execution start timestamp, when available. This is connector telemetry, not the source/instrument event time; facts observed in the world belong in linked sensorium-observation.v1 records.

completed_at.description: Connector-reported execution completion timestamp, when available. sensorium-core receipt of the connector response is recorded separately as connector/responded_at.

duration_ms.description: Connector-reported or sensorium-core-measured execution duration when available. Implementations SHOULD document which clock pair was used when precision matters.

issuer.description: Identity of the invoking party copied from the directive. participant/did:key is the sovereign identity axis; module_id identifies the local module when applicable.

module_id.description: Registered module id of the consumer, e.g. orbiplex.arca or orbiplex.dator.

participant/did:key.description: Participant identity on whose behalf the directive is issued. Preferred canonical form is participant:did:key:z...; direct did:key:z... remains accepted for local envelopes. Verifiers MUST normalize to participant:did:key:z... before comparing with the participant derived from issuer_delegation.principal_key.

node_id.description: Optional node identity associated with the issuer context.

action_id.description: Public action id invoked by the consumer.

connector/id.description: Connector module id selected by sensorium-core after allowlist resolution. Absent for directives rejected before connector selection.

allowlist/ref.description: Reference to the operator-signed allowlist entry used for admission.

policy/decision.description: Sensorium policy decision summary for admission, rejection, timeout, or failure.

retry/attempts.description: Number of execution attempts made for this directive outcome. Zero is valid for rejection before execution.

result/summary.description: Optional compact result summary. Full typed results remain action-specific and may be returned through sensorium-directive-result.v1 or artifact references.

observation/ids.description: Ids of sensorium-observation.v1 records emitted because the directive produced facts about the world. Empty or absent when no world-fact observation was produced.

artifacts.description: References to artifacts produced by the directive, such as stdout, stderr, generated files, or raw signal captures, using the minimal artifact-lane contract from proposal 045.

diagnostics.description: Structured diagnostics from sensorium-core or the connector. These are audit hints, not observation records.

issuer_delegation.description: Optional proposal-032 DelegationProof copied from the originating directive when the directive was signed by a proxy key. Outcome verification policy treats this as part of the directive audit chain; Sensorium v1 accepts only max_chain_depth=0.

audit/store.description: Optional host-owned audit sink reference. Directive outcomes are written to the Node-owned module store behind restricted sensorium.audit.* capabilities, not to local Agora observation topics.

capability.description: Restricted audit capability used to access this outcome record, e.g. sensorium.audit.read.

connector/dispatched_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when sensorium-core dispatched the admitted directive to the selected connector. Absent when the directive was rejected before connector selection.

connector/responded_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when sensorium-core received the terminal connector response. Absent when the connector did not respond before timing.timeout_ms expired.

directive/signature_digest.description: Optional sha256 digest of the originating directive signature bytes, using the canonical signature value exactly as verified by sensorium-core. Preserves audit linkage without duplicating the full signature in the outcome record.

artifactRef.description: Minimal artifact-lane reference. The artifact itself is stored outside this envelope and is addressed by a content or host-owned blob reference.

artifact/id.description: Content-addressed or host-owned artifact identifier. v1 accepts sha256:<base64url-or-hex> and memarium-blob:<opaque-id> forms.

role.description: Role of the artifact in the Sensorium exchange.

media_type.description: Optional media type of the referenced artifact.

delegationProof.description: Compact DelegationProof from proposal 032. In Sensorium v1 the proof may be present, but sub-delegation chains are not supported; max_chain_depth, when present, MUST be 0. Additional fields are tolerated for open-world forward compatibility; the canonical proof payload verified by implementations is limited to the proposal-032 compact proof fields.

proxy_key.description: did:key public key that signed the surrounding directive artifact.

principal_key.description: did:key public key of the principal participant. Verifiers derive participant:did:key:... from this value and compare it to the normalized issuer.participant/did:key.

grants.description: Operation/target grants authorised for the proxy key.

max_chain_depth.description: MVP restriction from proposal 032: sub-delegation chains are not accepted in Sensorium v1.

principal_signature.description: Base64url Ed25519 signature by principal_key over the canonical compact proof payload.
